reasoning: The Lean statement `IsOpen U ↔ ∀ x ∈ U, ¬ ClusterPt x (𝓟 Uᶜ)` is logically equivalent to Pugh's informal claim. By Mathlib's `mem_closure_iff_clusterPt`, `ClusterPt x (𝓟 Uᶜ)` is exactly `x ∈ closure Uᶜ`, which is the standard meaning of "x is a limit of Uᶜ" (every neighborhood of x meets Uᶜ, equivalently in metric spaces there is a sequence in Uᶜ converging to x). Thus the Lean statement reads "U is open iff no point of U lies in the closure of Uᶜ", equivalent to "U ⊆ interior U", i.e., U is open. The generalization from metric spaces (Pugh's context) to arbitrary topological spaces is harmless since the cluster-point characterization of closure holds in general topological spaces. The biconditional, quantifier structure, and set-theoretic framing all match.
